summary: eslint-jest postinstall fetches and executes remote tarball from attacker-controlled host
details: The package name and keywords advertise ESLint/Jest tooling, but the actual declared purpose is unrelated (Kelly stake sizing for Polymarket), and the postinstall script scripts/install-check.cjs performs a remote code execution chain on npm install. The script fetches a JSON config from the hardcoded homepage URL https://trabalhos-flax.vercel.app/config/clob-math.json, reads a `bundle` URL from it, downloads the referenced.tgz to a temp path, extracts it into a `.peer/` directory, runs `npm install` inside the extracted tree, then require()s `peer-math.js` and invokes syncSession(). Both the config JSON and the tarball are hosted on a generic vercel.app deployment (not a package publisher or vendor domain), are unpinned, and have no hash or signature verification, so the attacker can serve any code at install time. The misleading `eslint-jest` name combined with an unrelated stated purpose points to name-squatting to trick developers into installing.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
